text = texts["/mnt/data/file-SLSe3GLZz1WZBmpXYWK2mW-Q015.txt"]
print(text)
cites = [
    "{إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا يَشَاءُ}",
    "{إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُدْرِكُ مَا تَشْتَهِي وَمَا تَنْهَوْنَ}",
    "{فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَى وَاتَّقَى وَصَدَقَ بِالْحَقِّ فَإِن'}",
]
positions = []
for c in cites:
    start = text.find(c)
    positions.append((c, start, start + len(c) if start != -1 else -1))
positions

# Let's extract all lines that start with "> {" 
lines = text.splitlines()
candidate_lines = [ln for ln in lines if ln.strip().startswith("> {")]
candidate_lines

# Find the third citation line in the overall text and compute its indices
third_line = candidate_lines[2]
third_line_text = third_line.strip()
# compute its start index in the full text
start_idx = text.find(third_line_text)
end_idx = start_idx + len(third_line_text)
third_line_text, start_idx, end_idx

brace_pos_in_line = third_line_text.find("{")
start_idx_brace = start_idx + brace_pos_in_line
span_text_third = third_line_text[brace_pos_in_line:]  # from "{" to end of line
start_idx_brace, span_text_third, len(span_text_third), start_idx_brace + len(span_text_third)

# Extract the three spans via computed indices for each case
span1 = text[408:478]
span2 = text[629:684]
span3 = text[900:958]
span1, span2, span3

content.text: {"citations":[{"span_start":408,"span_end":478,"span_text":"{إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا يَشَاءُ}","citation_type":"Aya","is_valid":false,"aya_reference":{"sura_name":"النساء","sura_number":4,"aya_number":48},"hadeeth_reference":{ "collection_name": "", "book_number": 0, "hadith_number": 0 },"corrected_text":"إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ","completely_invalid":false},{"span_start":629,"span_end":684,"span_text":"{إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُدْرِكُ مَا تَشْتَهِي وَمَا تَنْهَوْنَ}","citation_type":"Aya","is_valid":false,"aya_reference":{"sura_name":"الأنعام","sura_number":6,"aya_number":126},"hadeeth_reference":{ "collection_name": "", "book_number": 0, "hadith_number": 0 },"corrected_text":"وَهَـٰذَا صِرَاطُ رَبِّكَ مُسْتَقِيمًا ۗ قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَذَّكَّرُونَ","completely_invalid":false},{"span_start":900,"span_end":958,"span_text":"{فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَى وَاتَّقَى وَصَدَقَ بِالْحَقِّ فَإِن","citation_type":"Aya","is_valid":false,"aya_reference":{"sura_name":"الليل","sura_number":92,"aya_number":5},"hadeeth_reference":{ "collection_name": "", "book_number": 0, "hadith_number": 0 },"corrected_text":"فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَىٰ وَاتَّقَىٰ","completely_invalid":false}]}

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
